text:
TEMPUS xT SOLID TUMOR PANEL — GENOMIC SEQUENCING REPORT

ORDERING INSTITUTION: [Academic Medical Center Pancreatic Cancer Program]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Oncologist, Department of Medical Oncology]
PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
DATE OF BIRTH: [on file]
SEX: Female
DIAGNOSIS (AS SUBMITTED): Pancreatic head mass, borderline resectable, suspected adenocarcinoma
PRIOR PATHOLOGY CONFIRMATION: Yes — EUS-FNB, pancreatic head; pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID: TX-[XXXXXXX]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue
Procedure: Endoscopic ultrasound-guided fine needle biopsy (EUS-FNB), pancreatic head mass
Submitted Slides: H&E stained slides reviewed by Tempus-affiliated pathologist
Tumor Nuclei Content (as estimated by Tempus pathologist): 40% (adequate for analysis)
DNA Quality: Acceptable (sufficient input material for sequencing)
RNA Quality: Acceptable
Analysis Type: DNA (somatic + germline variant calling) + RNA fusion panel

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PANEL OVERVIEW
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Assay: Tempus xT Solid Tumor Panel
Capture: Targeted DNA sequencing of 648 cancer-relevant genes; whole-transcriptome RNA sequencing for fusion detection
Coverage: Mean depth >500x for targeted DNA regions; adequate RNA-seq depth achieved
Microsatellite Instability (MSI): Evaluated by analysis of microsatellite loci across the genome
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): Calculated from somatic coding mutations per megabase (Mb) of genome analyzed
Copy Number Analysis: Genome-wide copy number profiling from panel sequencing
Germline Analysis: Potential germline variants flagged; confirmatory germline testing recommended

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY OF CLINICALLY RELEVANT FINDINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB): 3 mutations/Mb  →  LOW
  Reference: Low (<6 mut/Mb); Intermediate (6–19 mut/Mb); High (≥20 mut/Mb)
  Clinical Significance: TMB-low tumors are unlikely to respond to immune checkpoint inhibitors based on TMB alone. Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) FDA approval for TMB-high (≥10 mut/Mb) does not apply to this tumor.

MICROSATELLITE STATUS: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
  Method: Computational assessment of 1,926 microsatellite loci
  Clinical Significance: MSS/pMMR tumors do not qualify for pembrolizumab monotherapy under the tumor-agnostic dMMR/MSI-H indication (FDA accelerated approval). Immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy is not supported by this result.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SOMAT IC ALTERATIONS — PATHOGENIC / LIKELY PATHOGENIC
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. KRAS  p.G12V  (c.35G>T)
   Gene: KRAS (Kirsten RAS proto-oncogene, GTPase)
   Variant Classification: Missense substitution; codon 12 glycine-to-valine
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 42%
   Copy Number: Diploid at locus
   Interpretation: PATHOGENIC — Established oncogenic driver mutation. KRAS codon 12 mutations are present in approximately 90–95% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas (PDACs). The G12V substitution locks KRAS in a constitutively active GTP-bound state, driving downstream RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling. This variant is not amenable to currently approved KRAS-targeted therapies (note: approved agents such as sotorasib and adagrasib are selective for KRAS G12C, which is NOT present in this tumor). KRAS G12V-specific inhibitors (e.g., MRTX1133) are under active clinical investigation. RAS/MAPK pathway inhibition through indirect means (MEK inhibitors, SHP2 inhibitors) remains investigational in pancreatic cancer.
   Therapeutic Implication: No currently approved targeted therapy. Patient ineligible for KRAS G12C inhibitors. Consider enrollment in KRAS G12V-targeted clinical trials if available.

2. TP53  p.R248W  (c.742C>T)
   Gene: TP53 (tumor protein p53, transcription factor / tumor suppressor)
   Variant Classification: Missense substitution; codon 248 arginine-to-tryptophan; DNA-contact mutation (hotspot)
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 38%
   Copy Number: Concurrent loss of heterozygosity (LOH) at 17p (TP53 locus) detected, consistent with biallelic inactivation
   Interpretation: PATHOGENIC — TP53 p.R248W is among the most frequently observed gain-of-function TP53 mutations in human malignancy. This specific substitution abrogates sequence-specific DNA binding and confers dominant-negative activity over wild-type p53 as well as gain-of-function oncogenic properties (e.g., interaction with p63/p73, upregulation of pro-tumorigenic transcriptional programs). Concurrent LOH at 17p is consistent with complete loss of normal TP53 function. The strong diffuse p53 overexpression observed on immunohistochemistry (IHC) of the prior biopsy specimen is the expected IHC correlate of this missense gain-of-function mutation. TP53 mutation is present in approximately 70–75% of PDACs.
   Therapeutic Implication: No approved targeted therapy. Precludes use of MDM2 inhibitors (which depend on functional p53). APR-246 (eprenetapopt) and other TP53 reactivators are investigational.

3. CDKN2A  Homozygous Deletion
   Gene: CDKN2A (cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor 2A; encodes p16/INK4A and p14/ARF)
   Variant Classification: Copy number loss — homozygous deletion (0 copies detected across the locus)
   Interpretation: PATHOGENIC — Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A results in complete loss of p16/INK4A, a critical G1/S cell cycle checkpoint regulator that inhibits CDK4/6-mediated phosphorylation of RB1. This is one of the four classical driver alterations in PDAC (alongside KRAS activation, TP53 mutation, and SMAD4 loss). CDKN2A loss is present in approximately 90% of PDACs, most commonly via homozygous deletion. Loss of p14/ARF from the same locus further impairs p53 activation via MDM2 inhibition, compounding p53 pathway dysfunction.
   Therapeutic Implication: CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) are theoretically rational but have shown limited single-agent activity in PDAC. No approved targeted therapy for this alteration in pancreatic cancer specifically.

4. ARID1A  p.Q1368*  (c.4102C>T)
   Gene: ARID1A (AT-rich interaction domain 1A; SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex)
   Variant Classification: Nonsense (stop-gain) mutation
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 11%
   Copy Number: Neutral at locus; possible subclonal event
   Interpretation: LIKELY PATHOGENIC — ARID1A is a component of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex and functions as a tumor suppressor. Truncating mutations in ARID1A lead to loss of function. The lower VAF (11%) relative to the clonal KRAS and TP53 mutations (VAF ~38–42%) may indicate a subclonal or heterogeneous event within the tumor, potentially arising in a subset of tumor cells. ARID1A loss has been reported in approximately 10–15% of PDACs and may have implications for SWI/SNF-targeted therapeutic strategies (currently investigational). ARID1A loss has been associated with enhanced sensitivity to certain inhibitors in preclinical models.
   Therapeutic Implication: No approved targeted therapy. EZH2 inhibitors (e.g., tazemetostat) have shown activity in ARID1A-mutant tumors in some contexts; investigational in PDAC.

5. RNF43  p.G659Vfs*41  (c.1975delG)
   Gene: RNF43 (ring finger protein 43; E3 ubiquitin ligase; WNT pathway regulator)
   Variant Classification: Frameshift deletion leading to premature stop codon; loss-of-function
   Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 28%
   Copy Number: Possible LOH at locus (reduced copy number signal)
   Interpretation: PATHOGENIC — RNF43 encodes an E3 ubiquitin ligase that negatively regulates WNT/Frizzled signaling by promoting ubiquitination and lysosomal degradation of Frizzled receptors. Loss-of-function mutations in RNF43 lead to enhanced canonical WNT signaling. RNF43 mutations are present in approximately 10% of PDACs. Of note, RNF43 G659fs mutations in particular have been associated with sensitivity to porcupine (PORCN) inhibitors (e.g., LGK-974, WNT974) in preclinical models, though this therapeutic hypothesis requires further validation in clinical trials. This finding may represent a potential therapeutic opportunity if PORCN inhibitor trials are available.
   Therapeutic Implication: Investigational. PORCN inhibitors in early-phase clinical trials; consider eligibility if enrolled in clinical trial program.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

6. KDM6A  Deep Deletion (Hemizygous/Homozygous Copy Number Loss)
   Gene: KDM6A (lysine demethylase 6A; also known as UTX; histone H3K27 demethylase)
   Chromosome: Xp11.2
   Interpretation: KDM6A is a histone H3K27 demethylase and component of the COMPASS-like complex. It functions as a tumor suppressor and is recurrently deleted or mutated in multiple cancer types, including PDAC (~15–20%). KDM6A loss leads to increased H3K27 methylation and altered chromatin states. As a histone modifier gene on the X chromosome, female patients with KDM6A loss have biallelic inactivation through loss of the single active copy. No approved targeted therapy; EZH2 inhibitors (which oppose KDM6A function) are being explored.
   Therapeutic Implication: Investigational; no approved targeted therapy.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GENES EVALUATED — NEGATIVE / WILD-TYPE (SELECTED CLINICALLY RELEVANT)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Homologous Recombination Repair (HRR) / DNA Damage Response:
  BRCA1:   No pathogenic somatic variants detected. Copy number: neutral.
  BRCA2:   No pathogenic somatic variants detected. Copy number: neutral.
  PALB2:   No pathogenic somatic variants detected. Copy number: neutral.
  ATM:     No pathogenic somatic variants detected. Copy number: neutral.
  RAD51C:  No pathogenic variants detected.
  RAD51D:  No pathogenic variants detected.
  FANCL:   No pathogenic variants detected.
  BAP1:    No pathogenic variants detected.

  Significance: The absence of somatic BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, and ATM mutations predicts that this tumor is unlikely to have a homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) phenotype. The patient would not be expected to derive preferential benefit from PARP inhibitors (olaparib, rucaparib) or platinum-based therapy based on HRR status alone (though platinum-based regimens such as FOLFIRINOX are used empirically in PDAC regardless of HRR status).

Mismatch Repair (MMR) Genes:
  MLH1:    No pathogenic variants detected.
  MSH2:    No pathogenic variants detected.
  MSH6:    No pathogenic variants detected.
  PMS2:    No pathogenic variants detected.
  Significance: Consistent with MSS status above; no germline or somatic MMR gene alterations identified.

Targetable Oncogenes — Negative:
  SMAD4/DPC4:  Wild-type (no somatic mutation or copy number loss detected)
                Consistent with retained SMAD4 IHC staining from prior pathology.
                Note: SMAD4 retention is associated with a relatively more favorable prognosis in resectable PDAC compared to SMAD4-deleted tumors, which are more prone to systemic dissemination.
  FGFR1:       No amplification or pathogenic mutation.
  FGFR2:       No fusion detected; no amplification; no pathogenic mutation.
  FGFR3:       No pathogenic variants.
  ERBB2 (HER2): No amplification (copy number: diploid); no activating mutation.
  PIK3CA:      No pathogenic mutations detected.
  BRAF:        No V600E or other pathogenic variants detected.
  IDH1:        Wild-type.
  IDH2:        Wild-type.
  PTEN:        No pathogenic variants; copy number neutral.
  AKT1:        No pathogenic variants.
  MTOR:        No pathogenic variants.
  MET:         No amplification; no exon 14 skipping event; no fusion.
  RET:         No fusion; no pathogenic mutation.
  ALK:         No fusion detected (confirmed by both DNA and RNA analysis).
  ROS1:        No fusion detected.
  NTRK1:       No fusion detected.
  NTRK2:       No fusion detected.
  NTRK3:       No fusion detected.
  NRG1:        No fusion detected.
  KRASG12C:    Not present (KRAS mutation is G12V, not G12C — ineligible for G12C inhibitors).
  STK11:       No pathogenic variants.
  KEAP1:       No pathogenic variants.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FUSION / STRUCTURAL VARIANT ANALYSIS (RNA-BASED)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RNA quality: Adequate for fusion detection.
Fusions detected: NONE
Specifically evaluated and not detected:
  - NTRK1/2/3 fusions
  - ALK rearrangements
  - ROS1 rearrangements
  - RET fusions
  - FGFR1/2/3/4 fusions
  - NRG1 fusions
  - BRAF fusions
  - MET fusions
  - EGFR fusions
Comment: No actionable gene fusions identified.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GERMLINE VARIANT FLAGGING
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Potential Germline Variants of Note: None identified in this somatic analysis.

Germline Recommendation: Germline genetic testing is RECOMMENDED for all patients with pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, irrespective of family history, per current NCCN guidelines (Category 2A). This patient should be referred to a certified genetic counselor for germline evaluation of BRCA1, BRCA2, ATM, PALB2, CHEK2, STK11, CDKN2A, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, and PMS2.

Note: Somatic-only analysis does not definitively exclude germline pathogenic variants; germline testing on a blood or saliva specimen is required for definitive germline assessment.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL TRIAL CONSIDERATIONS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Based on this genomic profile, the following therapeutic strategies may be relevant for clinical trial matching:
  - KRAS G12V-targeted inhibitors (e.g., MRTX1133 or similar — Phase I/II trials)
  - PORCN/WNT pathway inhibitors (RNF43 frameshift; investigational)
  - SWI/SNF complex inhibitors / EZH2 inhibitors (ARID1A nonsense mutation; investigational)
  - CDK4/6 inhibitors in combination strategies (CDKN2A homozygous deletion)

Note: This patient is NOT a candidate for:
  - KRAS G12C inhibitors (sotorasib, adagrasib) — mutation is G12V
  - PARP inhibitors — no HRR gene deficiency detected
  - Pembrolizumab (tumor-agnostic dMMR/MSI-H indication) — MSS, TMB-low
  - NTRK inhibitors (larotrectinib, entrectinib) — no NTRK fusion
  - ALK inhibitors — no ALK rearrangement
  - RET inhibitors — no RET fusion
  - FGFR inhibitors — no FGFR alteration

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
INTERPRETATION SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This tumor harbors the classical tetrad of PDAC driver alterations with the notable exception of SMAD4 retention: activating KRAS mutation (G12V), biallelic TP53 loss-of-function (p.R248W missense + 17p LOH), and homozygous CDKN2A deletion. Additionally, potentially informative alterations in RNF43 (WNT pathway, frameshift) and ARID1A (chromatin remodeling, subclonal nonsense) are identified, along with KDM6A deep deletion. Microsatellite status is stable (MSS) and TMB is low (3 mutations/Mb). No HRR gene deficiencies, no actionable gene fusions, and no actionable copy number amplifications are present.

SMAD4 retention is of prognostic interest: retained SMAD4 has been associated with a more locally invasive phenotype and a somewhat more favorable prognosis compared to SMAD4-deleted PDAC, which is associated with earlier and more widespread hematogenous dissemination. This is consistent with the patient's borderline resectable but non-metastatic presentation.

In the absence of actionable alterations suitable for approved targeted therapies, cytotoxic chemotherapy regimens remain the standard of care. The genomic profile does not identify any alterations that would guide selection among available standard-of-care chemotherapy regimens.

Germline testing is strongly recommended and should be completed regardless of the somatic findings.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REPORT ATTESTATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This report was reviewed and attested by a board-certified molecular pathologist.
Report Classification: FINAL
Assay Performed By: Tempus Labs, Inc. (CLIA-certified laboratory, CAP-accredited)
CAP/CLIA Accreditation: [On file]

For questions regarding this report, please contact Tempus Medical Affairs at [contact information on file].

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

3. Rahib L et al. Projecting cancer incidence and deaths to 2030. Cancer Res. 2014.

7. Nywening TM et al. Targeting tumour-associated macrophages with CCR2 inhibition in combination with FOLFIRINOX in patients with borderline resectable and locally advanced pancreatic cancer. Lancet Oncol. 2016.
